title: What Tinnitus Teaches About the Nature of Perception
phrases: "Attention is the most undervalued resource you have. Everything else follows from where you place it."
"You are not a problem to be solved. You are a process to be witnessed."
"There is no version of growth that doesn't involve the dissolution of something you thought was permanent."
"The algorithm of your attention determines the landscape of your experience."
interjections: And this is the part nobody talks about.
researchers: Richard Davidson (neuroscience of meditation, emotional styles)

title: The Spiritual Dimension of Living with Unwanted Sound
phrases: "The algorithm of your attention determines the landscape of your experience."
"Every moment of genuine attention is a small act of liberation."
"Your nervous system doesn't care about your philosophy. It cares about what happened at three years old."
"At a certain depth of inquiry, the distinction between psychology and philosophy dissolves entirely."
"The breath doesn't need your management. It needs your companionship."

title: How Tinnitus Becomes a Doorway to Present-Moment Awareness
phrases: "There is no version of growth that doesn't involve the dissolution of something you thought was permanent."
"You are not a problem to be solved. You are a process to be witnessed."
"The most important things in life cannot be understood - only experienced."
"The paradox of acceptance is that nothing changes until you stop demanding that it does."

title: The Ancient Practice of Listening to Inner Sound
